chief_complaint: A 35-year-old female Nepali immigrant presented with sudden neurological deficits, including dizziness, pale appearance, diaphoresis, right-upper and bilateral-lower extremity paralysis, and impaired eye and mouth opening, 11 hours post-Cesarean delivery.
history: The patient had no prior medical or psychiatric history. She developed sudden neurological deficits on postoperative Day 0 following an uncomplicated Cesarean delivery. Extensive workup was unremarkable. Neurological deficits resolved within hours after ammonia inhalant stimulation.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: right-upper and bilateral-lower extremity paralysis with impaired eye and mouth opening.
labs: Computed tomography head: unremarkable. Computed tomography angiography head/neck: unremarkable. Magnetic resonance imaging brain: unremarkable.
